g.title: Front View of Completed Game Controller
g.desc: A front view showing the fully assembled game controller. The controller's flat wooden body is shaped like a wide butterfly with two curved handles. On the left side is a circular area for directional inputs marked with the numbers 1 through 4 and arrows. On the right is a group of six circular buttons labeled 5 through 10. Below the center logo, there is a horizontal row of four circular buttons labeled 11 through 14. At the top center notch, a black rectangular connector is plugged in, with six colored wires (black, red, yellow, green, blue, and purple) extending out to the left. A text warning in the top right corner reads, 'Do not push buttons too hard.'

g.title: Game Controller Inventory List
g.desc: This image lists the parts needed for the project: one 'Top' and one 'Bottom' piece (flat laser-cut plywood), two 1/2 inch brass screws, two 'Large Spacers' and two 'Small Spacers' (small wooden rings of varying sizes), one flexible 'Jumper Wire' with plastic tips, and one 'Custom PCBA' (a flat circuit board with electronic components).

g.title: Controller Component Identification and Layout
g.desc: This component guide displays the parts needed for assembly. There are two large plywood pieces shaped like a game controller; the 'Top' piece has eight small rectangular cutouts, and the 'Bottom' piece has six cutouts with a central pill-shaped slot near the bottom edge. Also included is a large, solid rectangular circuit board (PCBA) and a flat ribbon of several parallel jumper wires with a plastic connector at one end. You will find two pairs of thin wooden strips: two 'Large Spacers' (long with tabs on the ends) and two 'Small Spacers' (shorter with tabs). Finally, identify two small 1/2-inch brass screws.

g.title: Small Spacer component identification
g.desc: This component is a small cross-shaped piece of 5mm laser-cut plywood. It consists of a horizontal rectangular body with a small square tab protruding from the center of both the top and bottom edges. You should find two of these identical spacers in your kit.

g.title: Large Spacer Wood Component
g.desc: Find two identical long, thin plywood pieces. One end has a wide rectangular block near the tip. The long middle section is a narrow beam. The opposite end finishes with a cross-like shape, featuring two small tabs that stick out from the top and bottom edges.

g.title: Controller Top Wooden Plate
g.desc: A large, flat, 3mm thick wooden piece shaped like a game controller with two rounded handles. Each side has four cutouts for identification: two vertical rectangular slots (top and bottom), a small square slot in the middle, and a small circular hole just below the square.

g.title: Long Rectangular PCBA Module
g.desc: Locate a single, long and narrow rectangular circuit board with smooth, rounded corners. As a Printed Circuit Board Assembly (PCBA), it will feel rigid and have various small electronic components soldered to its surface. This module is significantly longer than it is wide.

g.title: Five-Conductor Ribbon Jumper Wire
g.desc: This component is a single bundle of five thin, flexible wires joined together side-by-side to form a flat ribbon. On one end, all five wires are secured into a single wide, rectangular plastic connector. On the other end, the wires separate into five individual single-pin connectors. You can identify this part by feeling the flat, ribbed texture of the wires and finding the contrast between the one large solid connector at one end and the five small, separate connector tips at the other.

g.title: Large Symmetrical Bottom Plate
g.desc: A single 3mm thick laser-cut wooden piece shaped like a wide, rounded bowtie or hourglass. It features several distinct tactile markers for orientation: a horizontal capsule-shaped slot is located in the lower center. Near the two upper rounded lobes are tall vertical rectangular slots. Near the two lower lobes are small square slots. On both the left and right sides, between the top and bottom slots, is a small circular hole positioned just above another small square slot.

g.title: Two 1/2 Inch Brass Screws
g.desc: Locate two small brass screws. Each screw has a round head with a single slot and a threaded shaft. You can identify these by measuring the shaft length, which is exactly one-half inch from the flat underside of the head to the tip.

g.title: Insert small spacers into main base notches
g.desc: Identify the large, flat wooden base piece which has a curved shape similar to a wide hourglass or game controller. Find two small wooden spacers shaped like a plus sign, each with a small rectangular tab on one edge. Locate the two small square holes on the top of the base piece, positioned near the center of each rounded end. Insert the tab of each cross-shaped spacer into these square holes so that they stand up vertically from the base.

g.title: Assemble PCBA and Connect Jumper Wire
g.desc: Identify the kidney-shaped plywood base plate and two large spacers, which are long, thin plywood strips with tabs. Insert the bottom tabs of these spacers into the four rectangular slots on the base plate. Next, take the PCBA, a rectangular board with raised buttons on its surface, and fit its side notches onto the middle tabs of the installed spacers. Finally, take the jumper wire ribbon and plug its black rectangular connector into the set of five metal pins on the right side of the PCBA.

g.title: Attaching the Controller Top Panel
g.desc: Identify the top wooden panel by its hourglass shape and engraved button markings. Place it over the base assembly, which contains the circuit board and its colorful ribbon cable. Align the two small circular holes on the left and right sides of the top panel with the wooden support posts on the base. Secure the top panel by driving two brass screws into these holes using a screwdriver.

g.title: Assembly Complete! Final Controller Overview
g.desc: The final image shows the completed game controller assembly. It is an hourglass-shaped device made by sandwiching electronic components between two laser-cut wooden plates. On the top surface, you will feel various buttons, switches, and the heads of the brass screws. A flat, multi-colored ribbon cable should be securely plugged into the back center of the controller. Confirm that all screws are tightened so the wooden plates are firm and do not wiggle. Your physical build is now finished.
